activities_projects.title: Journey of a Shirt Diagram
activities_projects.objective: Trace the path from cotton farming to market
steps: Draw a flowchart showing steps: cotton → ginning → spinning → weaving → garment making → market
Label each step clearly
Write one sentence describing what happens at each stage
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand the stages involved in making a shirt

activities_projects.title: Role‑Play: Market Participants
activities_projects.objective: Understand roles of different people in the market chain
steps: Assign roles: farmer, trader, weaver, factory worker, exporter, retail buyer
Act out buying and selling at each step
Discuss how profits and prices change along the chain
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students empathize with different economic roles

activities_projects.title: Fair Trade Poster
activities_projects.objective: Explore the concept of fair wages and trade
steps: Discuss what fair trade means
Create a poster showing examples of fair trade practices
Write a slogan about fair wages for workers

activities_projects.title: Profit Comparison Chart
steps: List profits of worker, exporter, and businessperson according to the chapter
Draw bar graph or pie chart
Write observations about inequality in profits
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students interpret data and understand inequality

activities_projects.title: Interview a Local Seller
activities_projects.objective: Connect classroom learning with real market practices
steps: Interview a local shopkeeper about how they price clothes
Ask about costs and profits
Write a short report of your findings
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students link textbook concepts to real life
activities_projects.evaluation: Quality of questions and report (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Market Vocabulary Cards
activities_projects.objective: Reinforce key terms from the chapter
steps: Write terms like ginning, exporter, profit, retail, wholesale
On reverse, write definitions
Exchange with classmates to quiz each other

activities_projects.title: Daily Life vs Market Chain
activities_projects.objective: Compare students’ daily purchases with the shirt market chain
steps: List things you buy regularly
Discuss how these products might reach you
Write similarities and differences with the shirt’s journey
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students relate markets to everyday life

activities_projects.title: Creative Story: A Shirt’s Journey
steps: Write a short story from the point of view of a shirt
Include all major stages of production and markets
Describe challenges faced by people involved
activities_projects.evaluation: Story structure and content accuracy (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Class Debate: Who Gains Most?
steps: Divide class into groups (workers, exporters, buyers)
Each group argues why their participant gains most in the shirt market
Vote on persuasive arguments
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students develop reasoning and discussion skills
activities_projects.evaluation: Quality of arguments and teamwork (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Mini Quiz on Market Concepts
steps: Prepare 10 questions from the chapter
Quiz classmates in pairs
Record and compare scores
